examples.title: Basic Producer-Consumer with Semaphores
examples.description: Classical solution using counting semaphores and mutex for bounded buffer
examples.explanation: This implementation uses two counting semaphores (empty and full) to coordinate buffer access and a mutex to ensure atomic buffer modifications. Producers wait for empty slots before producing; consumers wait for filled slots before consuming. The semaphores handle blocking automatically, while the mutex prevents race conditions on buffer indices.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(N) for buffer storage, O(1) for synchronization primitives

examples.title: Producer-Consumer with Condition Variables (Pthreads)
examples.description: Implementation using mutex and condition variables for blocking wait/wakeup
examples.explanation: Uses condition variables for blocking. Producers wait on not_full condition when buffer is full; consumers wait on not_empty when empty. The while loop (not if) handles spurious wakeups. Signal wakes one waiting thread; broadcast wakes all. This approach avoids busy-waiting and is efficient for blocking synchronization.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) amortized for produce/consume; O(1) for wait/signal

examples.title: Lock-Free SPSC Circular Buffer
examples.description: Single-producer single-consumer lock-free implementation using atomic CAS
examples.explanation: Lock-free SPSC using atomic operations. No locks needed because only one producer writes and only one consumer reads - no race on data slots. Uses simple check-then-act with atomics. True lock-free uses CAS loop for ABA prevention. Advantages: no kernel calls, low latency. Disadvantages: busy-waiting, only SPSC, complex for general case.
